task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless hosted execution intent create-request preview that consumes the denied server-side admission payload and planned intent-store boundary, then returns a rejected durable-intent create request shape. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel/action, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, admission tokens, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, Redis/Postgres connections, durable intent persistence, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, API keys, admission tokens, vault handles, or quota reservation identifiers.

Hosted intent create request must stay rejected until admission is allowed, vault handles exist, quota is reserved, worker lease exists, durable intent store is connected, and idempotent commit storage exists.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this create-request preview defines a future durable intent input contract and is not real durable persistence or execution authorization.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing execution admission payload, execution intent-store boundary, worker lease boundary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse existing Pro queue preview payloads, domain admission payloads, API route handlers, web fetchJson helper, and preview-only gate panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic hosted intent create-request preview payload that composes admission and store blockers while reporting persistence and execution disabled.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values, admission tokens, vault handles, quota reservation ids, and durable intent ids are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned as real capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue tests prove the hosted intent create request remains rejected and composes admission, store, lease, and idempotency blockers.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed create-request preview and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the hosted intent create-request panel/action, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real auth enforcement, credential access, quota reservation, durable queue persistence, provider execution, worker execution, or billing mutation.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless hosted intent create-request preview endpoint.
Pro web shell can request and render a rejected durable-intent create request preview for the current graph run/job.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local create-request preview. It composes existing admission, intent-store, and worker-lease blockers without issuing tokens, accepting credentials, reserving quota, mutating billing, starting workers, connecting Redis/Postgres, persisting intents, calling providers, or writing results.
